items.title: Japan’s borrowing costs soar to 30-year high on debt fears
items.description: Worries over long-term spending plans help push bond yields to highest since 1996

items.title: Apple interest thrusts China’s CXMT into memory chip spotlight
items.description: Sharp turnaround for state-backed company central to Beijing’s AI supply chain efforts

items.title: US launches new wave of Iran strikes after attacks on tankers
items.description: Trump administration also revokes licence allowing Islamic republic’s oil sales, sending crude prices higher

items.title: Le Pen is posing as the victim of a politicised judiciary
items.description: The strategy might play well with the far-right leader’s core voters, but the French as a whole don’t buy it

items.title: French far-right leader Marine Le Pen says she will run for president
items.description: Politician sets up unprecedented election campaign next year after judges uphold her conviction for embezzlement

items.title: US revokes waiver allowing Iranian oil sales after tanker strikes in Strait of Hormuz
items.description: Qatari and Saudi ships among targets near Oman coast

items.title: IMF names Silvana Tenreyro as its next chief economist
items.description: Former Bank of England policymaker will be second woman to take on the role

items.title: Meta releases first image model since Zuckerberg’s AI overhaul
items.description: Muse Spark Image will be integrated into tech giant’s AI chatbot and its Instagram photo app

items.title: Trump threatens to remove all US troops from Europe
items.description: US president revives calls for his country to take over Greenland

items.title: Trump signals reversal of Turkey F-35 ban
items.description: US president strikes friendlier tone with Turkish counterpart than Nato allies

items.title: How Europe would fight without America
items.description: As Nato meets, the continent is starting to think about how to defend itself without the US
